title: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.title: How to Make Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice recipe
authentic Korean Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice — a beloved Korean classic

description: Patbingsu is a beloved Korean shaved ice dessert, perfect for cooling down on a hot day. This refreshing treat features a mountain of finely shaved ice, generously topped with sweet red beans and a delightful array of colorful additions. It offers a wonderful balance of textures and flavors, making it a truly iconic Korean dessert.

ingredients.notes: such as strawberries, mango, or banana, diced

instructions.title: Prepare the Sweet Red Beans (Pat)
instructions.text: Drain the pre-soaked red beans. In a pot, cover the beans with fresh water and bring to a boil. Boil for 10 minutes, then drain and rinse. Return beans to the pot with 4 cups of fresh water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 40-50 minutes, or until very tender. Most water should be absorbed.

instructions.title: Sweeten the Red Beans
instructions.text: Once the red beans are tender, add the sugar and salt to the pot. Stir well to dissolve the sugar. Continue to cook over low heat for another 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beans are thick and syrupy. Mash some of the beans with a spoon or potato masher for a creamier texture, leaving some whole for texture. Let cool completely.

instructions.title: Prepare the Shaved Ice
instructions.text: If using an ice shaver, place the frozen milk cubes into the machine and shave into fine, fluffy ice. If you do not have an ice shaver, you can blend the frozen milk cubes in a powerful blender until it forms a fine, snow-like consistency. Work quickly to prevent melting.

instructions.title: Assemble the Base
instructions.text: Divide the shaved ice evenly among 4 serving bowls. Gently mound the ice in the center of each bowl, creating a small peak. This forms the canvas for your delicious toppings.

instructions.title: Add the Toppings
instructions.text: Generously spoon the cooled sweet red beans over the shaved ice in each bowl. Arrange the cut injeolmi rice cakes and fresh fruit around the red beans. If desired, add mini mochi and a sprinkle of roasted peanut powder for extra flavor and texture.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Drizzle each serving with a generous amount of sweetened condensed milk. Serve immediately with a spoon, encouraging diners to mix the toppings and ice together as they eat. Enjoy this refreshing Korean dessert!

tips: For the fluffiest shaved ice, ensure your milk is completely frozen solid. You can also add a touch of sugar to the milk before freezing for a slightly sweeter ice.
Adjust the sweetness of the red beans to your preference. Some prefer a less sweet pat, while others like it very rich. Taste and adjust sugar as it cooks.
Patbingsu is best enjoyed immediately after assembly to prevent the ice from melting. Prepare all toppings in advance so you can quickly put it together.
article: Patbingsu Red Bean Shaved Ice is a beloved Korean Desserts dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
